title: Divisibility tests
topic_path: Module 2: Arithmetic operations including dividing by a fraction

$210879$ is divisible by $3$ if it can be divided by $3$ without leaving a remainder.

A number is divisible by $3$ if the sum of its digits is divisible by $3$. 

[[☃ explanation 1]]

Add the digits of $\color{\#6495ED}{210879}$:

 $ \color{\#6495ED}{2}+\color{\#6495ED}{1}+\color{\#6495ED}{0}+\color{\#6495ED}{8}+\color{\#6495ED}{7}+\color{\#6495ED}{9} = \color{\#28AE7B}{27} $

  If $\color{\#28AE7B}{27}$ is divisible by $3$, then $\color{\#6495ED}{210879}$ must also be divisible by $3$.
$\color{\#28AE7B}{27}$ is divisible by $3$, therefore $\blue{210879}$ must also be divisible by $3$.

A number is divisible by $4$ if the last two digits are divisible by $4$.

Is the value of the last two digits, $8$, divisible by $4$?
Yes, $\blue{8 \div 4 = 2}$, so $419108$ must also be divisible by $4$.
